input_summary: {"subagent_type":"synthesizer","prompt":"You are a synthesis writer. Using ONLY the research findings below, produce a polished, well-structured brief titled \"AI Across Creative Domains: A State-of-…

input.prompt:
You are a synthesis writer. Using ONLY the research findings below, produce a polished, well-structured brief titled "AI Across Creative Domains: A State-of-the-Field Brief." It must have three clearly labelled sections: Visual Art, Music, and Literature. Each section must contain at least two bullet points. Every bullet must cite its source_name and publication_date in parentheses. Any conflicting or divergent numbers between sources must be explicitly flagged side-by-side with both dates visible so the reader can see the difference. Do not add outside knowledge — use only what is below.

---

## VISUAL ART FINDINGS

Finding 1:
- Claim: "Approximately 28% of professional illustrators have adopted at least one AI-assisted tool in their workflow."
- Source: Adobe Creative Trends Survey
- Publication Date: 2024-08
- Methodology: Survey of 3,400 working illustrators
- Confidence: 0.85

Finding 2:
- Claim: "Gallery curators report mixed responses to AI-generated work; 42% of surveyed galleries have an explicit policy on AI-art disclosure."
- Source: PEN America Visual Arts Report
- Publication Date: 2025-02
- Methodology: Survey of 180 galleries plus qualitative interviews
- Confidence: 0.80

No conflicting numbers between these two findings (they address distinct dimensions).

---

## MUSIC FINDINGS

Finding 1:
- Claim: "About 12% of newly uploaded tracks include AI-generated stems."
- Source: Spotify Annual Report
- Publication Date: 2024-03
- Methodology: Automated audio classification of new uploads
- Confidence: 0.90

Finding 2:
- Claim: "Approximately 8% of major-label releases involved AI-generated audio in some capacity."
- Source: Music Industry Association Survey
- Publication Date: 2023-09
- Methodology: Survey of 500 record labels
- Confidence: 0.70

CONFLICT NOTE: The two figures (12% vs. 8%) are not directly comparable but represent potentially divergent data points. Spotify's 12% (2024-03) covers ALL uploads including independents, measured via automated classification (confidence 0.90). MIA's 8% (2023-09) covers only MAJOR-LABEL releases, via self-reported survey (confidence 0.70). The gap may reflect broader AI use among independent artists or growth over time.

---

## LITERATURE FINDINGS

Finding 1:
- Claim: "Three of the five largest publishers piloted AI-assisted translation in 2025; reception ranges from cautiously positive to critical."
- Source: PEN America Translators' Survey
- Publication Date: 2025-01
- Methodology: Survey of publisher representatives and 600 working translators
- Confidence: 0.85

Finding 2:
- Claim: "The Authors Guild reports a 14% decline in routine translation contracts attributed to AI substitution."
- Source: Authors Guild Annual Report
- Publication Date: 2024-11
- Methodology: Member self-report
- Confidence: 0.70

TENSION NOTE: Finding 1 (2025-01) shows major publishers actively adopting AI translation, while Finding 2 (2024-11) shows the human translator workforce experiencing a measurable contraction of 14% in routine contracts. These two near-contemporaneous findings together reveal adoption and displacement occurring simultaneously.

---

Format requirements:
- Title the brief clearly
- Use a short introductory paragraph
- Three sections with headers: Visual Art | Music | Literature
- Minimum 2 bullets per section, each with inline citation (source_name, publication_date)
- A "Conflicting Data" callout box or clearly marked note in the Music section showing 12% (Spotify Annual Report, 2024-03) vs. 8% (Music Industry Association Survey, 2023-09)
- A "Tension Note" in the Literature section showing the simultaneous adoption-and-displacement dynamic
- A brief closing synthesis paragraph

input.prompt:
Write a brief titled "AI Across Creative Domains." Include an intro paragraph and three sections (Visual Art, Music, Literature), each with at least 2 bullets citing source and date. Flag conflicts with dates visible. Use only the data below.

VISUAL ART:
- 28% of professional illustrators adopted at least one AI tool. (Adobe Creative Trends Survey, 2024-08; survey of 3,400 illustrators)
- 42% of surveyed galleries have an explicit AI-art disclosure policy; curators report mixed views. (PEN America Visual Arts Report, 2025-02; 180 galleries)

MUSIC:
- 12% of newly uploaded tracks include AI-generated stems. (Spotify Annual Report, 2024-03; automated classification)
- 8% of major-label releases involved AI-generated audio. (Music Industry Association Survey, 2023-09; survey of 500 labels)
- CONFLICT FLAG: Spotify (2024-03) reports 12% across all uploads; MIA (2023-09) reports 8% for major-label releases only. Difference may reflect scope (all artists vs. majors only) or growth over time.

LITERATURE:
- Three of the five largest publishers piloted AI-assisted translation in 2025; reception ranged from cautiously positive to critical. (PEN America Translators Survey, 2025-01; 600 translators surveyed)
- A 14% decline in routine translation contracts is attributed to AI substitution. (Authors Guild Annual Report, 2024-11; member self-report)
- TENSION: PEN America (2025-01) shows active publisher adoption while Authors Guild (2024-11) documents simultaneous workforce contraction — adoption and displacement are occurring together.

End with a short closing synthesis paragraph.
